core_mechanism: spectral/orthogonal-series expansion of a square-integrable random object in a complete orthonormal basis (here iterated-integral / polynomial-chaos modes), truncated by best N-term nonlinear (sparse) approximation that retains only the largest coefficients under a compressibility assumption
brief_reason: The abstract builds on Wiener-chaos expansions of predictable square-integrable processes and its central guarantee is achieving the best N-term 'chaoslet' approximation rate for compressible coefficients, i.e. sparse truncation of an orthogonal-basis series.
